purpose: Additive push-trigger behavior over two existing chokepoints (no new module directory): (1) app/pages/profile.tsx's broadcastProfile fans an immediate profile-announce to every active, non-archived 1:1 contact on a nickname/avatar edit, staggered like the S05 watcher's bulk sweep; (2) app/src/lib/pairing/pairingAck.ts's sendPairingAck (scanner side) and handlePairingAck (issuer side) each fire one immediate profile-announce to the freshly-paired contact at admission. Both reuse story 03's send.ts#sendProfileAnnounce verbatim (the sole outbound-construction/privacy chokepoint) and never touch story 04's receive.ts (a pushed announce is received by the existing announce arm unmodified).

public_api.name: ProfileAnnounceContactSnapshot
public_api.signature: type ProfileAnnounceContactSnapshot = { pubkeyHex: string; archived: boolean }
public_api.description: profile.tsx. Input shape to planProfileAnnounceFanout — mirrors ProfileHealWatcher.tsx's ContactSnapshot but scoped to this module (no cross-module import, per architecture.md's boundary rules).

public_api.name: ProfileAnnounceFanoutEntry
public_api.signature: type ProfileAnnounceFanoutEntry = { pubkeyHex: string; delayMs: number }
public_api.description: profile.tsx. One planned send: lowercased recipient + dispatch-time stagger delay.

public_api.name: PROFILE_EDIT_FANOUT_STAGGER_THRESHOLD
public_api.description: profile.tsx. Audience size above which sends are staggered instead of firing in the same tick. Independent constant (not imported from ProfileHealWatcher.tsx) but same value, for consistency.

public_api.name: PROFILE_EDIT_FANOUT_STAGGER_WINDOW_MS
public_api.description: profile.tsx. The window a staggered fan-out's sends are spread evenly across.

public_api.name: planProfileAnnounceFanout
public_api.description: profile.tsx. PURE. Filters to active/non-archived, case-folds pubkeyHex, and assigns stagger delays exactly like ProfileHealWatcher.tsx#planDueSweep's toSend computation. Archived contacts are excluded at THIS function's own boundary (not a downstream filter), so the exclusion is directly assertable (VQ-S06-005).

public_api.name: ExecuteProfileAnnounceFanoutParams
public_api.signature: type ExecuteProfileAnnounceFanoutParams = { ndk: NDK; keys: ProfileSendKeys; localProfile: UserProfile; plan: ProfileAnnounceFanoutEntry[]; sendAnnounce?: typeof sendProfileAnnounce; scheduleDelay?: (fire: () => void, delayMs: number) => void }
public_api.description: profile.tsx. sendAnnounce/scheduleDelay are dependency-injection points for deterministic testing (default to the real send.ts seam and the real setTimeout).

public_api.name: executeProfileAnnounceFanout
public_api.description: profile.tsx. Synchronous, fire-and-forget: fires (or hands to scheduleDelay) one sendProfileAnnounce call per plan entry. Never awaited by its caller, never blocks/slows the profile-save UX; a single recipient's failure is swallowed (mirrors MarmotContext#publishProfileUpdate's per-group swallow).

public_api.name: handlePairingAck (opts.ndk addition)
public_api.signature: handlePairingAck(giftWrapEvent: GiftWrapEventLike, ownPrivateKeyHex: string, opts?: { nowSec?: number; ndk?: NDK }): Promise<HandlePairingAckResult>
public_api.description: pairingAck.ts. Additive, backward-compatible opts.ndk (new field on the existing optional opts param — NOT a new required param). On a FRESH 'admitted' outcome, when opts.ndk is supplied, fires one sendProfileAnnounce to the newly-admitted sender via a locally-derived ownPubkeyHex (nostrKeys.ts#derivePublicKeyHex(ownPrivateKeyHex) — no new param needed for the issuer's own pubkey) and the live local profile (storage.ts#readUserProfile()). When opts.ndk is omitted (every pre-story-06 call site), this is a pure no-op — the function's admission behavior and returned result are completely unaffected either way. Never fires on 'already-admitted' (idempotent replay).

public_api.name: sendPairingAck (push-trigger addition, no signature change)
public_api.description: pairingAck.ts. Additive: after the pairing-ack itself is successfully sent, fires one sendProfileAnnounce to issuerPubkeyHex using params.ndk/ownPubkeyHex/ownPrivateKeyHex (all already explicit params) and storage.ts#readUserProfile(). Swallowed independently — an announce failure never turns a successfully-sent ack into 'queued-for-retry'. No public signature change.

public_api.name: subscribeToWelcomes (call-site wiring only, no signature change)
public_api.signature: subscribeToWelcomes(pubkeyHex, marmotClient, ndk, signer, onGroupJoined, onJoinRequestReceived?, groupMemberPubkeys?, ownPrivateKeyHex?, onPairingAckReceived?): Promise<() => void>
public_api.description: welcomeSubscription.ts. Lead-authorized scope amendment (see notes). The ONLY change: its existing handlePairingAck(giftWrapEvent, ownPrivateKeyHex) call now passes a third argument, { ndk } — the SAME `ndk` this function already receives as its own param, already in closure scope at that exact call site. No new param on subscribeToWelcomes itself, no change to the kind-1059 subscription filter, no change to dispatch order or the Welcome/join-request fallthrough.

private_files: app/src/lib/marmot/welcomeSubscription.ts (scope amendment, lead-authorized after initial implementation — see notes; minimal, additive one-line wiring at the pre-existing handlePairingAck(...) call site only)
owned_data: None new. Reuses send.ts's outbound construction (no new store) and pairingAck.ts's existing in-memory pairingAckAdmissions map (unmodified).
dependencies_allowed: @/src/lib/dmProfile/send (sendProfileAnnounce, ProfileSendKeys) — the S03 privacy chokepoint, reused verbatim by both files
@/src/lib/ndkClient (connectNdk, dynamic import — profile.tsx only, matches this file's existing lazy-import pattern for drainPendingIntents/getSignEvent)
@/src/lib/storage (readUserProfile — pairingAck.ts only, to read the current local profile for the push-trigger announce without adding a new param that would require editing an out-of-scope caller)

@/src/lib/nostrKeys (derivePublicKeyHex, added to pairingAck.ts's existing import of hexToBytes from the same module — derives the issuer's own pubkeyHex from ownPrivateKeyHex, which handlePairingAck already receives, so no new param is needed)
dependencies_forbidden: app/src/lib/dmProfile/receive.ts (a pushed announce is received by the EXISTING announce arm unmodified — no import, no gate re-implementation)
app/src/lib/dmProfile/kinds.ts (no new kind; only send.ts constructs the wire payload)
app/src/components/ProfileHealWatcher.tsx (no cross-module dependency edge — profile.tsx defines its own independent stagger constants rather than importing the watcher's)

app/pages/add.tsx and app/src/lib/processContactInput.ts and app/src/lib/pairing/pendingIntent.ts (scanner-side admission already completes in these files before sendPairingAck is ever called; this story's scanner-side hook lives entirely inside sendPairingAck's own body, per stories.json's explicit files list for S06)
events_emitted: kind-1059 gift wrap, inner kind DM_PROFILE_ANNOUNCE_KIND (via the unmodified send.ts seam) — one per active/non-archived contact on edit, one per freshly-admitted contact on pairing

seam.note: handlePairingAck's admission block (pairingAck.ts) is shared textually with S07's §10.1 importCard fix, which lands after this story in the SAME function body, directly below this story's announce-send call (see the 'Step 10' comment landed in the file). S07 must add importCard(senderHex, decoded.profile) AFTER the announce call without reordering or removing it.

notes: RESOLVED (was a flagged scope-boundary gap, now closed by lead-authorized scope amendment): the initial S06 implementation built and unit-tested the issuer-side announce fully (handlePairingAck's new optional opts.ndk), but stories.json's files list at that time was exactly [app/pages/profile.tsx, app/src/lib/pairing/pairingAck.ts] — no third file — leaving the feature inert in the running app, since handlePairingAck's sole real caller, app/src/lib/marmot/welcomeSubscription.ts#subscribeToWelcomes (~line 122), already had `ndk` in scope at its handlePairingAck(...) call site but did not pass it. The lead reviewed this finding and explicitly authorized adding welcomeSubscription.ts to this story's scope for a MINIMAL, additive change: pass the function's own already-in-scope `ndk` as a third argument, `{ ndk }`, to the existing handlePairingAck(...) call — no change to the kind-1059 subscription filter, dispatch order, or Welcome/join-request fallthrough (verified by a source-scan regression test, mirroring S05's AC-WATCH-2 isolation style, plus a call-through spy proving the exact new argument). The issuer-side push-trigger announce is now live end-to-end, not merely unit-tested in isolation.

readUserProfile()/derivePublicKeyHex() were the two additions needed inside handlePairingAck/sendPairingAck to avoid widening either function's REQUIRED param surface: readUserProfile() (storage.ts) supplies the current local profile without a new required param threading through pendingIntent.ts/welcomeSubscription.ts; derivePublicKeyHex() (nostrKeys.ts, already imported in this file for hexToBytes) supplies the issuer's own pubkeyHex from the ownPrivateKeyHex handlePairingAck already receives. Both are read-only utility calls, not a re-implementation of any wrap/publish/gate logic. The welcomeSubscription.ts amendment above only had to add the OPTIONAL opts.ndk pass-through — no further param threading was needed.
